name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-15
title: extract_bash_targets supports only a few write verbs; coverage gaps for python -c, dd, tar, ln, install
description: hooks/scope-guard.py extract_bash_targets() detects redirects (> >>), tee, sed -i, cp/mv, rm, touch, mkdir. It does NOT detect: dd of=PATH, tar -xf to a directory, ln -s creating files inside repo, install -m, awk -i inplace, python -c "open('PATH','w')", any redirect through a heredoc to a file, or any of the many writing operations available via python/perl/ruby one-liners. A determined agent (or accidental misuse) can write inside the repo via any of these without scope-guard catching it. Spec invariant scope is 'conservative' (line 262: 'conservative') so this is documented behavior, but expanding the verb set or adding a 'PATH=' fast-path may strengthen the guarantee.

history.note: Deferred: too large/complex for housekeeping waves. Requires dedicated design cycle. Filed for future roadmap consideration.
